personal.name: Greg Lucas
personal.title: Faculty Chair · Academic Technology & Innovative Delivery Models
personal.summary: Higher-education technology leader with 15+ years driving academic technology strategy, LMS optimization, AI integration, and scalable online delivery models. Expertise in leading cross-functional teams to optimize learning management systems (including transitions from legacy platforms like Blackboard to custom LMS like Halo at GCU), manage continuous delivery/release cycles, oversee LTI integrations and functional testing, collaborate with vendors on roadmaps, and implement AI-enabled solutions for enhanced student success and operational efficiency. Founder of pAIgeBreaker and LMSBreaker (MoltALP) — AI-native, agentic platforms with LTI compatibility that personalize learning, automate content generation, and support adaptive ecosystems. Proven in translating institutional priorities into tactical implementations, stakeholder communication, and data-driven refinements in complex, enterprise environments.

core_competencies: Online Portfolio Support & Course Roll/Grade Pass-Back Processes

experience.title: Faculty & COLT Team Lead
highlights: Operated within one of the largest online CBE and digital learning ecosystems, gaining deep fluency in large-scale academic technology infrastructure, delivery model evolution, and institutional priorities.
Promoted to COLT Team Lead (2018); mentored faculty, drove instructional quality, and ensured operational integrity of the online learning environment.
Awarded Team Lead of the Year (2020) for leadership in faculty development, technology adoption, and student success outcomes.
Advised on AI tool adoption, emerging platforms, and LMS best practices to support forward-looking technology integration.
Maintained active engagement in national conversations on academic technology and delivery innovation.

experience.title: Founder & Lead Architect
highlights: Founded and architected an AI-native learning platform that replaces traditional textbooks with a network of specialized AI agents, delivering personalized, competency-aligned content via LTI 1.3 integration.

Designed agentic pipeline with seven core AI agents (Outliner, Generator, Assessor, Scorer, Persona Router, Mastery Gate, Remediator) that orchestrate dynamic curriculum generation, adaptive difficulty, and mastery-based progression.
Built Learning Genome for portable student personalization, real-time remediation, institutional audit/controls, Bloom's/DOK tagging, and accreditation exports.
Achieved 70% cost reduction vs. legacy models while improving content relevance and engagement.
Developed integration roadmaps for pilots in higher education and K-12.

experience.title: Founder & Lead Architect
highlights: Developed MoltALP, an AI-native platform built to transform/replace traditional LMS with native agentic modules for discussion intelligence, course architecture, automated grading, early warnings, and auto-responses.
Focused on AI-orchestrated education with measurable outcomes in faculty efficiency and student engagement.

experience.title: Faculty Chair & Academic Innovation Leader
highlights: Served as executive-level innovation partner to college Deans and senior leadership, leading cross-college technology strategy, AI integration, pilot execution, and new delivery model development.

Part of the team driving the institution-wide transition from Blackboard to GCU's internally developed Halo LMS; contributed to the integration roadmap (including Banner components), faculty adoption strategy, and alignment with course competencies.
Chaired Academic Technology AI subcommittee and led New Innovative Online (NIO) initiative, identifying opportunities for emerging technologies and integrating AI/custom extensions.
Directed cross-college faculty teams of 30-50 members across multiple colleges with accountability for instructional quality, technology adoption, and strategic execution.
Partnered with Academic Web Services, Curriculum, and VP-level leaders to develop AI-powered student support tools, dashboards, and cross-platform integrations.
Oversaw pilots saving >$5M annually including AI-integrated curriculum (Engineering), immersive technologies (Sciences), and custom content replacing third-party dependencies.
Led implementation of Google AI Badges and other industry credentials; delivered workshops on AI in education, LMS innovation, and emerging technologies.
Collaborated on overseas enrollment strategy, analyzing trends and producing deliverables to inform technology/program roadmaps.

experience.title: Technology Operations
highlights: Developed systems thinking and precision execution skills in large-scale engineering and technology environments.

innovation_projects.name: pAIgeBreaker
innovation_projects.description: AI-native learning platform with 7-agent agentic pipeline replacing traditional textbooks. LTI 1.3 compatible, adaptive difficulty, mastery-based progression.

innovation_projects.name: LMSBreaker (MoltALP)
innovation_projects.description: AI-orchestrated LMS replacement with native agentic modules for course architecture, automated grading, early warning systems, and discussion intelligence.

innovation_projects.name: Flourish AI
innovation_projects.description: Ethical AI agents for student/professional support, demonstrating AI system design and cross-sector application.

awards_recognition: COLT Team Lead of the Year, SNHU (2020)
Featured in GCU publications on AI innovation, metaverse/virtual engagement, and career preparation technologies
Frequent presenter on AI-enabled learning ecosystems, responsible AI, and academic technology delivery models
target_industries: EdTech companies (LMS vendors, adaptive learning platforms)
